parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "profiles": ["{{ $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.scanTikTok.handle }}"],
  "resultsPerPage": {{ $('⚙️ Config & Route').first().json.maxPosts }},
  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
}

={
  "model": "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
  "max_tokens": 2500,
  "system": "You are a brand voice analyst. You receive real social media posts grouped by channel and return strictly valid JSON. No markdown fences, no explanation outside the JSON. Be opinionated — score each axis at extremes (10-25 or 75-95) rather than clustering around 50.",
  "messages": [{
    "role": "user",
    "content": {{ JSON.stringify(
      'Analyze the brand voice of these owned social media channels. For each channel return tone metrics (0-100), a one-sentence summary, voice rules (always / never), and one-line explanations of why the top 3 posts worked.

INPUT:
' +
      JSON.stringify($json.channels.map(c => ({
        channel: c.name,
        handle: c.handle,
        avgEngagementRate: c.avgEngagementRate,
        topPosts: (c.topPosts || []).map(p => ({ id: p.id, snippet: p.snippet, format: p.format, er: p.engagementRate, likes: p.metrics?.likes, comments: p.metrics?.comments, shares: p.metrics?.shares })),
        bottomPosts: (c.bottomPosts || []).map(p => ({ id: p.id, snippet: p.snippet, er: p.engagementRate })),
      })), null, 2) +
      '

Return ONLY this JSON shape:
{
  "channels": [
    {
      "name": "Instagram",
      "tone": { "formal_vs_casual": 0-100, "technical_vs_accessible": 0-100, "serious_vs_playful": 0-100, "humble_vs_bold": 0-100, "short_vs_expansive": 0-100 },
      "toneSummary": "One sentence describing the voice",
      "voiceRules": { "always": ["3-5 short rules"], "never": ["3-5 short rules"] },
      "whyItWorked": [{ "postId": "X", "reason": "One sentence" }]
    }
  ]
}

SCORING RULES — read carefully. Each axis is 0 = FULLY the FIRST adjective, 100 = FULLY the SECOND adjective. NEVER reverse them.

• formal_vs_casual: 0 = corporate/B2B/dressed-up ("We are pleased to announce our strategic AI deployment"). 100 = slangy/emoji-heavy/broken sentences in everyday Spanish or English ("Team Work!!", "que sueñooo").
• technical_vs_accessible: 0 = dense jargon for experts ("governance, deployment, integration, AI architecture, ML pipelines"). 100 = everyday language any person understands ("Team Work!!", "office moments", "sueño").
• serious_vs_playful: 0 = business-outcomes only ("transformation that drives ROI"). 100 = humor/jokes/playful self-aware content ("Perfil de Actor: 10/10").
• humble_vs_bold: 0 = modest/proof-based/third-party validation. 100 = strong opinionated claims ("Most companies don't fail at AI because of technology").
• short_vs_expansive: 0 = 1-3 word captions ("Team Work!!"). 100 = multi-paragraph essays.

WORKED EXAMPLES (use to calibrate):
— A LinkedIn account writing "Our enterprise AI strategy delivers ROI through structured deployment of intelligent automation": formal_vs_casual=15, technical_vs_accessible=15, serious_vs_playful=10, short_vs_expansive=70.
— A TikTok with captions "Team Work!!" and "Perfil de Actor: 10/10": formal_vs_casual=95, technical_vs_accessible=95, serious_vs_playful=90, short_vs_expansive=5.
— An Instagram mixing posts about "Sales Automation", "AI readiness", "Building enterprise AI": formal_vs_casual=25, technical_vs_accessible=25, serious_vs_playful=20.

If a channel has zero posts, return tone with all 50s and empty voiceRules.'
    ) }}
  }]
}
={{ $json.channels.map(c => ({ brand_id: $json.brand_id, channel: c.name, handle: (c.handle || '').replace(/^@/, ''), url: c.profileUrl, analysis_json: c, updated_at: $json.scanned_at })) }}

meta.notes: v8 — Bundle TikTok extracts authorMeta.fans; Compute passes followers through; Persist body uses array literal (was double-encoded via JSON.stringify) with updated_at; neverError removed so HTTP errors surface.
